key_insight: A two-stage evolutionary hyperparameter search can make PINN training more reliable by cheaply screening many configurations first and only fully training the most promising ones.
engineering_relevance: This improves the practicality of PINNs for PDE-constrained engineering workflows by reducing wasted compute and lowering sensitivity to difficult optimization settings.
method: Stage 1: run truncated, low-fidelity PINN trainings to evaluate many hyperparameter candidates as a black-box search problem.
Use evolutionary algorithms to explore mixed, non-differentiable hyperparameter spaces under noisy loss landscapes.
Stage 2: fully train the top-ranked candidates with standard gradient-based optimization to refine accuracy.
evidence_signal: The approach was evaluated on three PDE benchmarks—Advection, Klein-Gordon, and Helmholtz—and consistently reduced mean error versus standard training under fixed compute budgets.
watch_item: Results may depend on the quality of the low-fidelity proxy and the chosen budget, so poor screening settings could discard good configurations early.
paper_title: Evolutionary Two-Stage Hyperparameter Optimization Strategies for Physics-Informed Neural Networks
